artPrompt: The frozen instant of a coin suspended at the apex of its flip, splitting into two ghostly overlapping faces of itself, mid-air above an ashen prophet's upturned palm, neither landed, both glowing cold gold. A looming clock tower and guttering candles dissolve into the blood-dark gloom behind. One single light source halos the doubled coin, rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Schrödinger's Prophecy
text:
Prophecy (Predict heads AND tails simultaneously. Write both down. Do not flip the coin.)
The coin is now both heads and tails. You are both correct and incorrect. Resolve this by drawing a card (you were right) and putting a doom counter on the Doomsday Clock (you were also wrong).
While this prophecy remains unobserved on the stack, treat the Doomsday Clock as simultaneously at midnight and not at midnight. The table may choose to look, collapsing the wavefunction, at which point whatever is funnier happens.
flavorText: The prophet was both right and wrong, dead and alive, and — crucially — very smug about all four.
